free_text: Excitation functions of (p,xn) reactions on natural
tellurium at low energy cyclotron: relevance to the
production of medical radioisotope I-123
CS30 cyclotron at King Faisal
Specialized Hospital and Research Center.
High purity natural tellurium, more than 99.9%, natural
isotopic abundance in %-Te-120 0.096, Te-122 2.603,
Te-128 31.69 and Te-130 33.80, was used as target
material.
Thin target samples were prepared by electrodepositing
Te onto 12 mum thick Ti foils, more than 99.9%, of 8 mm
diameter. The deposited target was covered by 50 mum
thick pure al foil, more than 99.9%., to protect tham
and avoid any loss of Te or radioiodine. The sandwich
sample was Ti-Te-Al.
Two stacks (a total of 38 sandwich samples and several
10 mum thick Cu foils were used.
for beam current monitored
The detector absolute efficiency was about 42% while
its resolution was 1.9 keV at the 1.33 MeV gamma-line
of Co-60.
The total error in the measured cross section
10-15% was obtained by combining uncertainty on each
measured cross section value, by taking the square root
of the summation of the following contributing sources:
The uncertainty on number of target atoms.
The uncertainty on nuclear data.
The uncertainty on beam intensity.
The uncertainty on absolute efficiency
of the spectrometer.
Statistical and peak fitting uncertainty.
The integral yield of I-123 with its
radionuclide impurity I-121,124,126,128,130 were
calculated from the measured cross section data.
Table 3  from NRP,1,93,2006
